source.title: Connect a code source
source.subtitle: Shield needs at least one project to scan. Pick a GitHub repo, a folder on disk, or skip and add one later.
source.github_ready_body: Opens the repo picker — pick one or many, Shield will scan them on a schedule.
source.github_needs_connect_body: GitHub OAuth is configured but not connected — connect below to pick repos.
source.github_needs_setup_body: GitHub isn't configured yet — set up the OAuth client below to start picking repos.
source.github_open_picker_hint: Multi-select repos — Shield scans on a schedule.
source.local_btn_body: Browse the filesystem and pick any folder with lockfiles — works without OAuth.
source.skip_btn_body: You can add sources later from the Sources page.
source.existing_sources_note: You already have {count} source configured. You're free to add more or continue. | You already have {count} sources configured. You're free to add more or continue.
source.github_added_toast: Added {n} GitHub source. | Added {n} GitHub sources.
source.folders_added_toast: Added {n} folder source. | Added {n} folder sources.

oauth_setup.github_intro: Create an OAuth App on GitHub, then paste the credentials below. Shield uses these to read your lockfiles and (optionally) open fix PRs.
oauth_setup.redirect_uri_hint: Paste this exact value into GitHub's "Authorization callback URL" field when creating the OAuth App.
oauth_setup.waiting_for_connect: Waiting for GitHub to redirect back…
oauth_setup.popup_blocked: The popup was blocked. Allow popups for this site or click Connect again.
oauth_setup.connect_failed: Failed to start GitHub OAuth flow.
oauth_setup.connect_timeout: GitHub didn't finish connecting. Try the Connect button again.
oauth_setup.status_configured: OAuth credentials saved — not connected yet.
oauth_setup.github_intro_device: One-click connect through Shield's published GitHub App. No OAuth App registration needed.
oauth_setup.device_connect_hint: Opens github.com/login/device — paste the code and authorise.
oauth_setup.device_paste_intro: Paste this code at github.com/login/device. Shield is already opening the page in a new tab and the code is copied to your clipboard.
oauth_setup.device_expired: The code expired before authorisation completed. Click Connect again.
oauth_setup.advanced_summary: Advanced: use your own OAuth App
oauth_setup.advanced_intro: Register a private GitHub OAuth App if you want Shield's API calls to count against your own quota instead of the shared default.
oauth_setup.disconnect_confirm: Disconnect GitHub? Future scans will fall back to anonymous (rate-limited) access.

external.paste_intro: Paste this code on {provider} to authorise the sign-in. We've copied it to your clipboard.
external.error_start: Couldn't start the sign-in. Try again.
external.error_denied: Authorisation was denied on the provider.
external.error_provider: The provider didn't respond. Try again.

needs_invite.title: Invite required
needs_invite.body: You're authenticated as {login} on {provider} — but no Shield user is linked to this identity. Ask the Shield admin to invite you using the details below.

register.title: Create the first admin
register.subtitle: Set a username and password to continue.
register.closed_body: New accounts are disabled on this instance. Ask an admin to invite you, or sign in below.

account.title: Account
account.two_factor_help: Protect your account by requiring a 6-digit code at sign-in.
account.two_factor_required_by_policy: An administrator has required 2FA on this server. Enable it to keep using the API.
account.recovery_codes_warning: Each code works once. Store them somewhere safe — they're the only way back in if you lose your device.

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.subtitle: Devices currently signed in to your account.
sessions.revoke_all_confirm: This signs out every device including this one. Continue?

tokens.title: API tokens
tokens.subtitle: Personal tokens for CI pipelines and scripts. Scoped, revocable, and shown once at creation.
tokens.empty: No API tokens yet. Create one to let a CI pipeline hit Shield without a cookie.

field.name: Name
field.source_filter_hint: Leave empty to allow every source the owning user can see.

reveal.title: Token created
reveal.warning: Copy this token now — it will never be shown again. If you lose it, revoke and create a new one.

error.name_required: Name and at least one scope are required.
error.copy_failed: Failed to copy token. Select it manually.

invite.section_intro: Send an email with a short-lived link. The invitee signs in with their code-host account — Shield never sees a password and never pre-creates an account.
invite.no_groups_hint: No groups yet — create one below to scope this invite.
invite.email_delivered: Email delivered. The invitee will receive a link to accept the invitation.
invite.email_skipped: Email skipped ({reason}). Relay this link manually:
invite.toast_created_no_email: Invitation created for {email} — email skipped ({reason}). Relay the link manually.
invite.github_section_intro: Pick a GitHub user from an organization you administer, or search any GitHub login. The invite is bound to their GitHub account — only they can claim it.
invite.github_not_connected: Connect GitHub to invite from your organizations.
invite.github_token_invalid: Your GitHub connection expired. Reconnect to reload organizations.
invite.github_org_empty: You aren't a member of any organizations on this token.
invite.github_members_empty: No public members on this page.
invite.github_toast_created_no_email: Invitation created for {login} — share the accept link manually.
invite.github_error_orgs_failed: Failed to load your GitHub organizations.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure how this Shield instance behaves.

section_access.title: Account & access
section_access.description: Who can sign in, and how identities map to Shield users.
section_access.single_user_hint_on: First-run shortcut — everything runs as a single admin without sign-in.
section_access.open_registration_hint: Anyone reaching /register can create an account. Invitations still work either way.

section_oauth.title: Code-host OAuth
section_oauth.description: Pair Shield with GitHub, Slack and Google to scan repos and deliver alerts. Each provider is independent.

section_alerts.title: Alerts
section_alerts.description: How much noise Shield is allowed to make, and for how long.
section_alerts.severity_floor_hint: Findings below this level are stored but never paged out.
section_alerts.retention_hint: Findings and notifications older than this are archived.

section_exposure.title: Public exposure
section_exposure.description: Read-only — these come from env vars set at boot. Edit your compose file to change them.

section_api.title: API
section_api.description: REST + OpenAPI surface used by the CLI, integrations and the web UI.
section_api.openapi_hint: When on, the schema is served at /swagger and the interactive UI is reachable.

section_auto_fix.title: Auto-fix
section_auto_fix.description: Configure automated bulk security-fix pull requests per source. Shield opens one PR grouping all fixable vulnerabilities.

accept_invite.title: Accept your Shield invitation
accept_invite.subtitle: You've been invited to a self-hosted Shield instance. Sign in with your code-host account to confirm it's you.
accept_invite.invalid_title: This invitation is no longer valid.
accept_invite.invalid_not_found: We couldn't find this invitation. The link may be malformed.
accept_invite.invalid_expired: The link has expired or been revoked. Ask the admin to send a new one.
accept_invite.invalid_missing_token: No invitation token in the URL.
accept_invite.invalid_network: We couldn't reach the Shield server. Try again in a minute.
accept_invite.intro: To accept, sign in with your code-host account. Shield will never see a password.
accept_invite.error_missing_ticket: Sign-in didn't return an acceptance ticket. Ask the admin to send a fresh invitation.
accept_invite.error_expired: This invitation expired or was revoked while signing in.
accept_invite.error_failed: We couldn't accept the invitation. Try signing in again.

install.title: Install Shield
install.body: Install Shield to your home screen for quicker launches and to receive push notifications on iOS.
install.ios_hint: On iOS, tap the Share button and choose "Add to Home Screen" to install.

push.card_subtitle: Send Shield alerts to this device even when the tab is closed.
push.unsupported: This browser doesn't support push notifications. Try Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or install Shield as an app on iOS 16.4+.
push.permission_denied: Notifications are blocked for this site in your browser settings.
push.permission_denied_title: Notifications are blocked for this site
push.permission_denied_explainer: Your browser remembers the deny decision and won't show the prompt again. You'll need to flip the setting manually and come back here.
push.ios_pwa_required: iOS requires Shield to be installed (Add to Home Screen) before push notifications can be enabled.

recovery.chrome: Click the lock / tune icon in the address bar → Site settings → Notifications → Allow. Then return to this tab.
recovery.edge: Click the lock icon in the address bar → Permissions for this site → Notifications → Allow. Then return to this tab.
recovery.firefox: Click the shield / lock icon in the address bar → Connection secure → More information → Permissions → Receive notifications → uncheck "Block". Then return to this tab.
recovery.safari: Safari → Preferences → Websites → Notifications → find this site → set to Allow. Then return to this tab.
recovery.generic: Open your browser's site permissions for this page and set Notifications to Allow. Then return to this tab.

push.test_sent_toast: Test dispatched to {n} device. | Test dispatched to {n} devices.
push.test_no_subscriptions_toast: No subscriptions on this account — subscribe first.
push.troubleshooting_body: Delivery is fire-and-forget — the server can't tell if your OS displayed the toast. If nothing appears:
push.troubleshooting_focus_assist: Windows: Settings → System → Notifications. Make sure Focus Assist / Do Not Disturb is off and this browser is allowed.
push.troubleshooting_macos_dnd: macOS: System Settings → Notifications. Confirm your browser is permitted and Focus is off.
push.troubleshooting_browser_perm: Browser permission may have been revoked since you subscribed — disconnect and re-enable to refresh.
push.subscribe_success_toast: This device will now receive push notifications.
push.enable_banner_body: Subscribe this device so high-severity findings reach you even when Shield isn't open.

findings.empty: No findings match the current filters.

bulk.ack_done: Acknowledged {n} findings. | Acknowledged {n} finding. | Acknowledged {n} findings.

error.impersonation_blocked: This action is blocked during impersonation. Exit impersonation first.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Admin actions recorded by the server: finding transitions, source / channel mutations, settings changes, OAuth connect / disconnect, invitations.
audit.empty: No audit entries match the current filters. Try clearing them to see the most recent admin actions across this Shield instance.
audit.undo_confirm: Undo {action} on {target}? The row will be restored to its pre-edit state.

channels.title: Channels
channels.validation_error: Please fill in the required fields.

feeds.title: Feeds
feeds.empty_body: Shield ingests advisories from OSV, GHSA and the npm registry; KEV and EPSS layer threat intel on top. Feeds register at boot — check the server logs if this list stays empty.

finding_detail.fix_not_available: No known fix is available for this advisory yet.
finding_detail.fix_files_changed: Applied fix ({files} file changed). | Applied fix ({files} files changed).
finding_detail.fix_source_unsupported: Source type does not support automatic fixes yet.
finding_detail.readonly_notice: You have read-only access to this source. Ask an admin for Triage permission to acknowledge, resolve or suppress findings.

notifications_view.title: Notifications
notifications_view.empty_body: Shield posts here when a scan fails, a feed goes quiet, or a watched package picks up a new anomaly. You can scope which severities reach you under Settings → Alerts.
notifications_view.view_pr_label: Open pull request in new tab

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Correlated security signal across Shield's own detectors and fail2ban hosts. fail2ban does the blocking — Shield observes, correlates, and surfaces the timeline.
security.no_events: No security events match the current filters. Either nothing's happening or the filter is too narrow — try clearing the source chip.
security.ban_count: {n} ban this session. | {n} bans this session.
security.no_ips: No IPs match the current filter.
security.no_hosts: No fail2ban host has shipped events yet. Configure your jails to action shield — see docs/security-fail2ban.md.

event_body.login_failed: Wrong password / 2FA code, or unknown account.
event_body.session_revoked_cookie_replay: A previously-valid session cookie was presented after revocation — possible session theft.
event_body.session_stale_cookie_presented: A session cookie outlived its row (admin wipe, table prune, or expired). Cookie was cleared on the response.
event_body.session_revoked: User or admin revoked the session — no further requests from this cookie will be accepted.
event_body.crawler_detected: Request matched a known crawler signature outside an allowlisted path.
event_body.rate_limit: Caller exceeded a configured request budget. Subsequent requests inside the window 429.
event_body.apitoken_created: A new API token was minted for this account.
event_body.apitoken_revoked: An API token was revoked — future uses will 401.
event_body.apitoken_used: An API token was presented and accepted.
event_body.password_changed: Account password was changed via the Account page.
event_body.twofactor_enrolled: Two-factor authentication was turned on for this account.
event_body.fail2ban_ban: An upstream fail2ban jail banned an IP and reported it to Shield.
event_body.fail2ban_unban: An upstream fail2ban jail lifted a ban.
event_body.fail2ban_found: fail2ban observed a match against one of its filters but did not ban yet.
event_body.ban_requested: An admin manually requested a fail2ban ban from the Security view.
event_body.fix_bulk_pr_opened: Shield opened a bulk-fix pull request on a code source.

sources.title: Sources
sources.empty_body: Pick a GitHub repo, point Shield at a local folder, or paste a config JSON to start scanning. Each source is rescanned on its own schedule.
sources.granted_note: Showing {n} source(s) granted to you. Ask an admin to expand access.
sources.github_add_error: Failed to add GitHub repo sources.
sources.refresh_success_no_login: No GitHub login bound to your account. Sign in via GitHub once to enable the mirror.
sources.refresh_success_none: Refreshed. No sources matched your GitHub orgs ({orgs}).
sources.refresh_error: Failed to refresh GitHub access. Check that your GitHub login has the read:org scope.
sources.github_connect_tooltip: Pick repos from your connected GitHub account
sources.github_connect_required_tooltip: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations first
sources.auto_fix_weekly_title: Shield will open a bulk fix PR once per week after scanning.
sources.auto_fix_scan_title: Shield will open a bulk fix PR after every scan (24 h cooldown).
sources.refresh_access_no_login: No GitHub login bound. Connect GitHub in Settings first.
sources.refresh_access_none: You don't have permission to scan this source.
sources.repo_picker_hint: Use the picker button below to select repositories from your connected account.
sources.folder_picker_hint: Use the picker button below to browse the server filesystem.

source_detail.delete_confirm: Delete source "{name}"? Snapshots and findings for this source will be deleted too.
source_detail.promote_error: Failed to promote to GitHub source.
source_detail.compare_pick_error: Pick two distinct snapshots to compare.
source_detail.compare_subtitle: Diff two snapshots to surface adds, removes, version bumps, and supply-chain anomalies.
source_detail.bulk_apply_confirm_production: I understand this targets a production source.
source_detail.bulk_apply_cooldown_tooltip: Bulk apply ran recently. Next eligible: {when}.
source_detail.bulk_apply_cooldown_notice: A bulk apply was opened recently. The next manual apply unlocks at {when}, but the server will still let this through if there are open findings newer than the last apply.
source_detail.bulk_apply_cooldown_error: Bulk apply on cooldown until {when}. Use "Force" to override.
source_detail.field_min_package_age_hint: Warns when a suggested fix version is younger than this. Advisory-listed fixes ship anyway. 0 disables the warning. Default 48.

accept_invite_view.title: Accept your Shield invitation
accept_invite_view.subtitle: Sign in with your code-host account to claim it.
accept_invite_view.invalid_title: This invitation is no longer valid.
accept_invite_view.error_not_found: We couldn't find this invitation. The link may be malformed.
accept_invite_view.error_expired: The link has expired or been revoked. Ask the admin to send a new one.
accept_invite_view.error_missing_token: No invitation token in the URL.
accept_invite_view.error_network: We couldn't reach the Shield server. Try again in a minute.
accept_invite_view.signin_hint: To accept, sign in with your code-host account. Shield will never see a password.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_expired: The invitation expired or was revoked. Ask the admin for a fresh link.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_auth: We couldn't confirm your identity. Sign in with GitHub and try again.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_retry: We couldn't complete the invitation. Try again or ask the admin.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_ticket: Sign-in didn't return an acceptance ticket. Ask the admin to send a fresh invitation.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_generic: We couldn't accept the invitation. Try signing in again.
accept_invite_view.accept_error_revoked: This invitation expired or was revoked while signing in.

onboarding.welcome_subtitle: A self-hosted, cross-ecosystem dependency vulnerability watcher for the code you ship.
onboarding.welcome_steps_intro: Here's what we'll set up in the next few steps:
onboarding.welcome_step_source: A code source — a GitHub repo or a local folder with lockfiles. Shield watches it for new advisories.
onboarding.welcome_step_channel: An alert channel — where Shield pings you when a new vulnerability lands. Discord works in seconds.
onboarding.welcome_step_dashboard: The Dashboard — your at-a-glance view of every open finding, severity-graded and grouped by source.
onboarding.welcome_skip_note: You can skip any step and configure it later from the Sources or Channels pages.
onboarding.channel_subtitle: Where should Shield ping you when a new vulnerability lands? Discord is the fastest path — paste a webhook URL and test it in one click.
onboarding.webhook_hint: Create one in Discord under Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
onboarding.test_dispatched: Test alert dispatched — confirm it landed in your Discord channel.
onboarding.test_failed: Test send failed. The webhook may be invalid or the channel deleted.
onboarding.skip_inbox_title: Skip — I'll use the in-app inbox
onboarding.skip_inbox_body: Shield ships with an inbox channel by default; alerts will be visible in the Findings page even without an external channel.
onboarding.channel_count: You already have {n} channel configured. | You already have {n} channels configured.
onboarding.done_subtitle: Shield will pick up the rest from here. Here's what's already wired in:
onboarding.done_sources_none: None yet — add some from the Sources page.
onboarding.done_sources_ready: Watched for new advisories on a schedule.
onboarding.done_channels_none: None yet — alerts will sit in the in-app inbox.
onboarding.done_tip_title: Tip: scan a source on demand
onboarding.done_tip_body: Scheduled scans run hourly by default. To see findings immediately, open a source from the Sources page and click Scan now.
onboarding.webhook_url_help: Paste the full webhook URL from Discord channel settings.
onboarding.welcome_skip_hint: You can configure these later in Settings.

watches.title: Watched packages
watches.empty: Star a package to track its findings across all sources.

settings_oauth.slack_configure_summary: Configure Slack OAuth credentials for signin.

settings_view.gitlab_callback_hint: Set the redirect URI in your GitLab application to:
settings_view.bitbucket_callback_hint: Set the callback URL in your Bitbucket OAuth consumer to:
settings_view.forgejo_callback_hint: Set the redirect URI in your Forgejo OAuth application to:
settings_view.forgejo_host_hint: URL of your self-hosted Forgejo instance — change applies on save without restart.
settings_view.gitea_callback_hint: Set the redirect URI in your Gitea OAuth application to:
settings_view.gitea_host_hint: URL of your self-hosted Gitea instance — change applies on save without restart.
settings_view.codeberg_callback_hint: Set the redirect URI in your Codeberg OAuth application to:

folder_picker.title: Pick folder(s)
folder_picker.subtitle: Walk the server filesystem and pick folders with lockfiles to scan.

repo_picker.title: Pick repos from GitHub
repo_picker.subtitle: Multi-select repos the connected account can access.
repo_picker.empty_filter: No repos match the current filters.

access_view.title: Access
access_view.no_groups_hint: No groups yet — create one below to scope this invite.
access_view.email_tab_hint: Send an email with a short-lived link. The invitee signs in with their code-host account — Shield never sees a password and never pre-creates an account.
access_view.github_tab_hint: Pick a GitHub user from an organization you administer, or search any GitHub login. The invite is bound to their GitHub account — only they can claim it.
access_view.github_not_connected: Connect GitHub to invite from your organizations.
access_view.github_expired: Your GitHub connection expired. Reconnect to reload organizations.
access_view.org_none: You aren't a member of any organizations on this token.
access_view.members_none: No public members on this page.
access_view.invite_ready_label: Invitation ready. Share this link with the invitee:
access_view.invite_sent_manual: Invitation created for {recipient} — email skipped ({reason}). Relay the link manually.
access_view.invite_sent_manual_github: Invitation created for {login} — share the accept link manually.
access_view.copy_error: Couldn't copy — copy it manually from the field above.
access_view.role_promote_confirm: Promote {username} to {role}? The user will need to sign out and sign back in for the new permissions to apply.
access_view.role_demote_confirm: Demote {username} to {role}? All higher-tier permissions will be removed; they will need to sign out and sign back in.
access_view.role_change_failed: Could not change the user's role.
access_view.role_last_admin: Refused — this is the only remaining Admin. Promote another user first.
access_view.delete_group_confirm: Delete this group? All grants made via the group will be revoked.
access_view.add_member_error: Failed to add member (user may not exist or already in group).
access_view.grants_notice: Per-source grants are managed from the Source detail page. Open a source to grant Read or Triage to a user or group, or revoke an existing grant. Effective level for a user is the maximum of direct + every group they belong to.
access_view.access_levels_detail: {read} (view findings, snapshots, inventory) · {triage} (Read + ack/resolve/suppress + scan-now + apply-fix + update/delete source).

updates_view.title: Updates
updates_view.subtitle: Outdated direct dependencies across every source.
updates_view.empty_hint: Trigger a refresh to scan every enabled GitHub source against the package registry cache.
updates_view.apply_queued: Apply job queued — progress will stream here.
